title: passport-jwt Strategy with ignoreExpiration: true — expired JWTs are accepted forever

params.passDetail: The passport-jwt Strategy enforces expiry (ignoreExpiration is not true) — a token past its exp claim is rejected.
params.failDetail: The passport-jwt Strategy is constructed with ignoreExpiration: true, so the exp claim is NOT checked and expired tokens are accepted indefinitely. A token leaked or logged months ago still authenticates; revocation-by-expiry no longer works. Remove ignoreExpiration: true (the default false enforces expiry).
params.absentCallDetail: No passport-jwt Strategy construction in this scope; this rule does not apply here.
params.absentArgDetail: The Strategy does not set ignoreExpiration; the secure default (false) applies.

provenance.note: The passport-jwt twin of jwt-verify-ignore-expiration: ignoreExpiration: true is set to stop tokens expiring during development and left in, so the whole app accepts expired JWTs. Distinct SDK and constructor call site from the jsonwebtoken verify-side rule.
